Papers & Articles

  • Geographical Centralization Resilience in Ethereum’s Block-Building Paradigms by @syang, @boz1, Fei Wu, and Fan Zhang has been updated to include theoretical foundations that explain the structural reasons why Ethereum’s block-building paradigms are not geographically neutral, and identify which protocol knobs amplify or dampen these effects.
    • Prisoners of Geography 2.0: The Theory Behind Where Validators Want to Run by Fei Wu
    • Post by Fei Wu
  • When Frontrunning is Alchemy by @dataalways details how aggregate sandwich attack statistics can be massively distorted by low-liquidity token swaps, overstating bot profits and user losses.
  • Economic Security of VDF-Based Randomness Beacons: Models, Thresholds, and Design Guidelines by Zhenhang Shang and Kani Chen presents a formal framework for the economic security of VDF-based randomness beacons.
  • Blocks Are Dead. Long Live Blobs by Toni Wahrstätter explains how Block-in-Blobs (BiB) encodes transaction data into blobs, enabling validators to sample data rather than download full execution payloads.
    • Post by Toni Wahrstätter
  • Optimizing a $100B Market: Effects of Latency Reduction on ETH Staking Revenue by Moritz Grundei, Slobodan Sudaric-Hefner, Sajida Zouarhi, and Muriel Médard analyzes how reducing network latency increases validator revenue by extending the usable slot time and enabling higher-value bids from builders.

Posts & Threads

  • Thomas Coratger published a thread summarizing Unblocking faster finality with decoupled consensus.
